TASK: The game is crashing. Player and Enemy classes require arguments in __init__. Fix main.py to pass appropriate values (e.g. Player(100, 10), Enemy(50)) and ensure player has an attack method or the battle loop is corrected.
TASK: player.py and enemy.py need attack methods. player.attack(enemy) should reduce enemy health by player attack value. enemy.attack(player) should reduce player health by a fixed value (e.g. 5).

TASK: player.py is missing the attack method. Add a method attack(self, enemy) that reduces enemy.health by self.attack. In enemy.py, ensure there is an attack(self, player) method. In main.py, ensure Player(100, 10) and Enemy(50) are initialized correctly and the battle loop works.

TASK: Critical bug in main.py. The player.attack(enemy) call is failing with TypeError: int object is not callable. This is because player.attack is being treated as an attribute (the attack value) instead of a method. Check player.py and ensure the attack value and attack method do not have the same name, or rename the attribute to self.attack_power.

TASK: player.py needs a working attack method. It should reduce enemy.health by self.attack_power. Currently it is a pass. Also ensure main.py is updated to pass arguments correctly to Player(attack_power, health).

TASK: 1. Overwrite player.py with a complete Player class including __init__(self, attack_power, health) and attack(self, enemy) method. 2. Overwrite main.py with a main() that initializes Player(10, 100) and Enemy(50) and runs the battle loop.
TASK: Update main.py to use the new Player constructor: Player(attack_power, health). Example: Player(10, 100).
TASK: Use CREATE_FILE to overwrite main.py with the proper battle loop code. Player takes (10, 100) and Enemy takes (50).

TASK: Create a terminal-based RPG with 3 files: player.py (Player class with health/attack), enemy.py (Enemy class with health/attack), and main.py (Battle loop where they take turns until one dies). EVERYTHING MUST WORK IN ONE ITERATION.
TASK: Add a win condition message when the game ends in main.py.
